item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh dang! That makes the article even more epic. Thanks for that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought I was wrong when I saw the discussion area below in scp-cn, but I was relieved when I saw Zyn.
Zyn doesn't even have scp-cn Membership！(ｰｰ;)

item.title: the role of -j articles
item.content:encoded:
I have always wondered how -j articles fit in with the broader narrative of the foundation. I really like the idea that SCP-4463-j was written to neutralize SCP-4463. Joke SCP are written to make the fan base reject the original article they were based on. That's going to be my new headcannon for all joke scp. For the foundation even telling a joke has life or death stakes.
Now I'm going to go reread scp 106-j and see if I can't help neutralize the old man.

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Closed
item.content:encoded: Noting that the above constitutes malicious downvoting, and the user in question has received a site ban for this behavior which is essentially trolling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
-1

not because i don't like it but because if this gets deleted then we get to make shitty jokes

i like making shitty jokes

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
zyn's coolpage funpage

In all seriousness though, it's put together amazingly. I love meta wankery too much, and this just hits home. A definite +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I did not feel very strongly about this page until the addendum, it gave me a sense of dread, I also felt bad for that researcher. I enjoy meta SCPs, but they aren't always executed well, this one however, did a very good job, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In-universe Zyn Kiryu does speak some Chinese—she and her brother are half Chinese (mother) half Japanese (father).

Real-life Zyn took Chinese school classes in elementary school and forgot pretty much everything and now has a terrible American accent. ;-;

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow. This is awesome. It's a +1 for me.

P.S. I came here from the version of this written in Chinese and my first reaction to the third addendum was "Whatt???? Zyn speaks Chinese???"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of the very few meta articles I unabashedly love. The existential horror is so powerful, and one of the absolute best format screws.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Something something proper procedure, something something staff consensus

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Damn it Flops, that would have been a great opportunity for Zyn to straight up ban Smalls. Also very meta.
To everyone that read and enjoyed this — THANK YOU ALL SO MUCH! ❤️
I hate this (in the best way). It legitimately made my heart stop- probably the scariest SCP I've… ever read. Ever. Good work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The fake comment section at the end really spooked me - enough that I got jumpscared when I accidentally zoomed at a portion of the screen and it filled the whole screen.
I love this.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Meta stuff that refers to the existence of our website in or relating to the SCP universe really isn't my favourite, but this one is just done so damn well. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh I love this, I really really do.

item.title: Re: ?
item.content:encoded:
I think he meant something about the new feeling of uneasiness you feel when you read something as different and as wild as an SCP article, but it's all over again with this article. He's saying that he is re-feeling that old spooky shiver feeling from back in the days where we would watch spooky horror lets-play youtube videos.

Bottom line: It's original, impactive and spooky like 173 once was.

item.title: Re: +420
item.content:encoded: Thank you, your services are appreciated.
I've one-upped Crocket by getting this page to +420. Finally, I'll be remembered.

item.title: Beginning deletion vote at +400
item.content:encoded:
It looks like meta didn't destroy the fabric of the Wiki after all.

I was not expecting to ever get a +400, not to mention on this SCP. I'm blown away, thanks everyone!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It would have been pretty ironic if people didn't like it and it ended up deleted.

As the co-author of this work, I actually think it's a miracle that it survived in the first place! This was a very controversial article when it was first posted — the first few discussion pages are a testament to that — especially among established/veteran authors. In contrast, I have noticed that the majority of people inclined to upvote this, tend to be newer members and contributors. Maybe because the article is a meta-reflection of the new author experience, and (often-times) one's uphill battle to get their first creative work to stick on the SCP Wiki?

(As an aside — I would say that the Forum Post gimmick is what saved this article, more-or-less, even though that was initially regarded as low-effort and not in service of the storytelling. I think the opposite: this story of one man's fear of being forgotten would not fit in a conventional article, and the simple gimmick was used as a vehicle for more direct, emotional storytelling.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It would have been pretty ironic if people didn't like it and it ended up deleted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
its 2747 applied irl
+1 for meta screw

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellently done.

item.title: Stop
item.content:encoded:
Stop fucking with my mind.

Stop.

Are these even the forums anymore?

+1
This is so meta you guys like woah

item.title: Re: April Fools Day Edit!
item.content:encoded: So very well done. Very applause, very meta.

item.title: Re: April Fools Day Edit!
item.content:encoded: Epic!

item.title: Re: April Fools Day Edit!
item.content:encoded: in which i am forced to shitpost on some random woman's meta scp oh god please help

item.title: April Fools Day Edit!
item.content:encoded:
Please enjoy this VERY META April Fools edit.

The unedited, uncensored, and unabridged version of SCP-682 x SCP-777-J fan fiction can be found at [DATA LOST].

item.title: Pataphysics Department logo
item.content:encoded:
That spinning Foundation logo in the upper left of the screen is amazing. Shame I can't grab it as a gif image (':
Also, this was my first reading of a meta SCP, and because of that, I guess it was a lot more amazing to me then a lot of other's think

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Freaking brilliant. Maybe it's just my general appreciation for good meta writing, but…well, I love this to death. So many bonus points for the formatting and coding in the last section….

item.title: Re: The post is broken for me
item.content:encoded: did you read what you typed?

item.title: The post is broken for me
item.content:encoded: No idea why either. Just doesn't work; I have ███████ all typed out yet when I click "Post It" nothing happens

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not a fan, mostly because I hate pataphysics as a concept. It's difficult to properly phrase for me, but postmodern stuff about the relation between fiction and the author doesn't feel like it has a place in the foundationverse. The Foundation, while obviously full of silly concepts, is ultimately a fairly serious setting. The idea of 4th wall breaking is always gonna end up feeling incredibly goofy, both because it means the settings gods are a geeks on computers, and because it requires us engage in really intensive SoDB to pretend like a bunch of electronic symbols can somehow form a conscious entity.

Of course I could just be stodgy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't wanna say this is the absolute last meta thing I'll ever upvote, but we are quickly running out of room for novelty. Even this piece retreads a lot of old ground, but the characterization was interesting enough to feel worthwhile.

item.title: Update
item.content:encoded: With ghosthorses' new SCP-4463 (as featured in this article) and >250 net upvotes, the long circle is now complete. Check it out!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: hi hadi.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i just used the forum because you said it looked neat

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know, I originally liked this, heck, you could even say I loved it. But I have pondered on it for almost all day now, and I have changed my vote to a novote.

Rant
– hide block

As someone who came up with the idea to use the forum gimmick to tell a meta story, as the person who created the character Resesrcher Smalls, and as a person who is referenced in the article itself, I feel as if my vote was biased and didn't actually fully reflect my thoughts on the article as a whole.

For one, the idea is very unoriginal as many people have pointed out, but my main reason for no voting is the fact that there's no reason for me to get invested in Smalls. We don't get nearly enough time with the character for us to sympathize with him by the end. If we spent more time with smalls, I'd probably like this a bit more.

Everyone has said it better than I ever could have as to why I'm novoting. But I feel as if it's just using the forum to gain a few more newbie upvotes, rather than tell an engaging story.

I'm glad the article exists. I'm glad I'm referenced in the article. The article just feels incomplete.

TL;DR: Hmm. This has potential, but I feel like this is just WAY too meta. Meta articles are too played out these days. Novote for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1! This REALLY fits my headcanon, where deleting articles causes the described objects to disappear in-universe! Although, I believe that it is retroactive (i.e. the object never existed in the first place)!
What do you mean about 173?
Congrats on the 200 positives. Poor Researcher Smalls but it's in deletion range.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Meh. This is really well written and formatted. And I really like the idea of exploring the idea of the nowhere/neverwas that these deleted things go. But ugh, I hate meta so much. I just hate it so much.

Damn, I hate having to upvote this. +1

item.title: Re: SCP-001
item.content:encoded: no its a different concept

item.title: SCP-001
item.content:encoded:
I am running on zero hours sleep so im too tired to check if someone else has already said this but isn't this essentially a rehash and/or extension of SCP-001?

I mean have this as an extension sure but you are kinda stepping on some shoes here with this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The CSS was removed by flops

idk why

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is it just me or did Researcher Smalls rise in Karma from the last time I've seen this?

Also, what happened to the cool CSS that was on this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is brilliant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not usually a huge fan of meta articles. This one, however, was extraordinary. I enjoyed the fact that the deletion of coldposts was an SCP, but when I got to the end of the third addendum my jaw literally dropped (and that never happens!). Congrats on a great article!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This sounds like a club I can get behind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
pls no, i has no tiem, pls let me slep ;(

Unless it's something like presidency of the "Everyone Eats Snacks Together" club, in which case, sign me up, I'll bring goldfish crackers and chips and a heckton of fruit juice

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: zyn 4 president 2020

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Who's Zyx?

ETA: in case it's not clear, I'm joking here. :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP 3309 is definitely Zyx XD peace! You're awesome! The best staff I've known across the internet!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This has absolutely been done before. Many concepts have been. What matters is execution, IMO, and if people like to upvote a concept they've already seen before then that's their discretion.

In my opinion, the use of the forum in this SCP is better suited to this concept than the Talloran article.

EDIT: I'm not saying the execution here is excellent. I'm not saying that at all. I'm just saying the forum use is justified.

item.title: Existential Crisis
item.content:encoded:
I didn't need an existential crisis.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Kinda annoyed me how there’s a part of a tale that literally does the exact same “do not roleplay” thing, but since it’s a tale it got ignored.

http://www.scp-wiki.net/only-once-away-my-son/offset/4

There’s also coolpage funpage… Joreth’s page… if people are upvoting this just because of that scene I want them to know that this has already been done before, like, 3 times.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wooaah… this one is good.

And I just realised, I had ano idea for an SCP, and this could be very very connected….
